description_long: This object is a classic British police telephone box, most notably recognized as the TARDIS from Doctor Who. It features a tall, rectangular structure painted in a deep blue color, with a flat roof topped by a small, vented lantern. The front side displays two panelled doors, one of which has a white sign with instructions, and above the doors is a black sign with white text reading 'POLICE PUBLIC CALL BOX.' The upper sections of the doors and sides have small, multi-pane windows with white frames. The booth is constructed from what appears to be painted wood or metal, with glass window panels and a solid, robust base. The overall design is utilitarian yet iconic, intended to be both weather-resistant and highly visible in an urban environment.
description: A tall, blue, rectangular police telephone box with paneled doors, multi-pane windows, and prominent 'POLICE PUBLIC CALL BOX' signage. The structure is solid, with a vented lantern on top and a white instruction sign on one door.
description_view_0: Front view showing two panelled doors, a white instruction sign on the left door, and the 'POLICE PUBLIC CALL BOX' sign above the windows.
description_view_1: Rear view with no signage or instruction panel, only the blue paneled surface and upper windows visible.
description_view_2: Side view similar to the rear, with blue panels and upper windows, lacking any signage or instruction panel.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again showing blue panels and upper windows, with the 'POLICE PUBLIC CALL BOX' sign visible above.
attribute_window_configuration: two multi-pane rectangular windows on each side near the top
attribute_signage_or_labeling: Black and white 'POLICE PUBLIC CALL BOX' sign above doors, white instruction sign on left door
